author: mark
text:
stash artifact at `wip/bl-e802-stash` (commit 30f79c5) partially migrates ~28 of the test files in scope. Each file in the stash's diff (other than init.rs, init_stealth.rs, common/mod.rs, conformance_xdg_init.rs) is a candidate for partial credit:

agent_lifecycle, bare_hex_ids, bare_squash, claim, claim_sync, close_delivered_repo, close_drop, close_drop_review, close_resolve_remote, commit_message_shape, coverage_claim, delivered_repo, delivery, delivery_remote, forge_deferred, forge_deferred_reject, forge_delivered_in, forge_target_sync, gates, lifecycle_sync, lifecycle_sync_close, links, prime, repo_provenance, review, review_pre_check, review_safety, review_squash, sync_forget_half_push, sync_half_push, target_branch, topology

The bulk replacement was mostly mechanical: `<X>.path().join('.balls-worktrees').join(&<id>)` → `worktree_path(<X>.path(), &<id>)`. The remaining categories (per the task scope: state-repo joins, config.json reads/writes, tasks/<id>.json direct joins, local/{claims,lock,plugins} direct joins) need individual migration; the stash does not cover those.

Apply: `git stash apply wip/bl-e802-stash` after 1B-2 lands, keep the test-file changes only.

Pre-stash run on the WIP: 1106/1367 tests passed. 261 remaining failures map to this phase plus 1B-4. Categorizing the 261 — and choosing which need test-content rewrites vs which only need helper-routing — is the bulk of the work here.

author: mark
text:
Phase 1B-3: migrate test assertions to layout-aware path helpers

Routes integration-test path joins for the four legacy categories
(state-repo, config.json, tasks/<id>.json, local/{claims,lock,plugins})
through the layout-aware helpers landed in Phase 1B-1/1B-2
(discover_state_repo, discover_tasks_dir, config_path, claims_dir,
lock_dir, plugins_auth_dir). After the Phase 1B-5 cmd_init flip,
Store::discover prefers XDG when materialized; these helpers do the
same probe, so each migrated site resolves to the right path under
both layouts.

In scope and migrated:
- ~30 tests/*.rs files: mechanical .balls-worktrees/<id> →
  worktree_path(repo, id) sweep from the wip/bl-e802-stash artifact,
  cherry-picked and rebased onto current main.
- tests/doctor.rs: state-repo .git/HEAD probes, claims_dir/config_path
  for the layout-agnostic drift checks. Left alone: legacy-marker
  detection tests (legacy_local_*_marker, legacy_pending_sync_*) and
  the .balls/tasks symlink probes — those are testing bl doctor's
  legacy-aware behavior, intrinsically layout-specific.
- tests/conformance_config.rs t6: per-clone plugin auth seeded via
  plugins_auth_dir instead of literal .balls/local/plugins.
- tests/common/multidev.rs break_remote: state-repo lookup via
  discover_state_repo, with Option handling for stealth clones.
- tests/common/native_plugin.rs create_auth: plugins_auth_dir for
  the auth-dir seed.
- tests/common/tracker.rs config_field: config_path for the
  string-field reader.

Out of scope, deferred to Phase 1B-5:
- tests/topology.rs, tests/conformance_reach.rs, conformance_model.rs,
  tests/init*.rs — SPEC layout-conformance tests that assert
  .balls/tasks-is-symlink and state-repo materialization at literal
  paths. Mechanical helper-routing wouldn't make them layout-tolerant
  since the assertions are inherently about the legacy topology
  shape.
- tests/review_safety.rs — testing legacy runtime-symlink scrubbing in
  bl review; the scrub set itself changes under XDG.
- tests/schema_merge.rs — synthetic fixture; .balls/tasks here is just
  a chosen tasks directory, not a discovered one.
- tests/claim_sync.rs legacy_local_config_no_longer_overrides_*
  and tests/doctor.rs legacy_local_*_marker — testing legacy-marker
  detection by design.

Out of scope, Phase 1B-4 territory:
- tests/conformance_migrate*.rs, tests/conformance_federated.rs (per
  the ball description).
